inputs.label: Feed Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of feed ingredient; affects nutritional value and cost. Based on animal nutrition standards (NRC).

inputs.label: Quantity per Day
inputs.businessContext: Daily feed consumption per animal or per herd. Derived from production records.

inputs.label: Price per kg
inputs.businessContext: Market price of feed ingredient. Sourced from commodity exchanges or supplier contracts.

inputs.label: Number of Animals
inputs.businessContext: Total animals being fed. Essential for scaling cost.

inputs.label: Feeding Days
inputs.businessContext: Duration of feeding period. Typically a production cycle or a year.

inputs.label: Waste Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Estimated feed waste due to spoilage, spillage, or inefficiency. Based on industry benchmarks (e.g., 5-10% typical).

inputs.label: Labor Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Hourly wage for feeding labor. Includes wages and benefits.

inputs.label: Labor Hours per Day
inputs.businessContext: Daily labor hours dedicated to feeding operations.

inputs.label: Transport Cost per kg
inputs.businessContext: Cost to transport feed from supplier to farm. Varies by distance and mode.

inputs.label: Storage Cost per kg
inputs.businessContext: Cost for storing feed (silos, warehouses). Includes depreciation and utilities.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data accuracy. Used for risk adjustment.

thresholds.wastePercentage > 10: High waste percentage; consider improving feed management.
thresholds.transportCostPerKg > 0.1: Transport cost high; consider local suppliers.

suggestedActions: Reduce waste by improving feed storage and handling.
Optimize labor schedule to reduce hours or automate feeding.
Negotiate bulk transport rates or source locally.
Review feed formulation for cost-effective alternatives.
